channel.title: Posts on Old Trunk
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Old Trunk

item.title: Doubly Linked Lists
item.description: Doubly linked list is a complex type of linked list in which a node contains a pointer to the previous as well as the next node in the sequence.

item.title: Linked Lists
item.description: A linked-list is a sequence of data structures which are connected together via links.

item.title: Graph
item.description: A Graph is a non-linear data structure consisting of vertices and edges.

item.title: Balanced Brackets
item.description: Balanced Brackets is a pretty cool algorithm that exemplify how to use Stack data structures.

item.title: Binary Search Tree
item.description: In computer science, a binary search tree (BST), also called an ordered or sorted binary tree, is a rooted binary tree data structure with the key of each internal node being greater than all the keys in the respective node’s left subtree and less than the ones in its right subtree.

item.title: Zero MQ
item.description: Zeromq is a message library that allow developers to design “brokerless” application.

item.title: Jakarta EE MicroProfile - Metrics
item.description: MicroProfile is an initiative of Java community that brings the power of Java EE to the microservices world.

item.title: Jakarta EE MicroProfile
item.description: Eclipse MicroProfile is optimized for developers to build microservices and cloud-native applications using enterprise Java.

item.title: JVM Profiling
item.description: Visual VM # open up visual vm visualvm --jdkhome "/Home/OpenJDK/jdk-11.

item.title: Date & Datetime
item.description: Java Date & Datetime API samples. Get day of week for a given year.

item.title: Min & Max Array Value
item.description: public class Solution { public int max(int [] array) { int max = 0; for(int i=0; i<array.

item.title: Lambda & DynanoDB & Gateway
item.description: Deploying AWS Lambda, API Gateway and DynanoDB using AWS Serverless Application Model(SAM).

item.title: Lambda & S3
item.description: Deploying AWS Lambda using Java and AWS Serverless Application Model(SAM).

item.title: Hash Tables
item.description: Hash tables are excellent for quick storage and retrieval of data based on key-value pairs.

item.title: VertX + Elasticsearch
item.description: Log aggreagator on top of Eclipse VertX Elasticsearch

item.title: Linear Searching - Minor Element
item.description: public class MinorElement { public static void main(String[] args) { int a[] = {110, 244, 37, 49, 510}; System.

item.title: Git - Quick References
item.description: useful git commands for day-to-day devs Stash # clean up GIT stack git stash clear # stash working branch git stash # apply stashed changes git stash apply Fetch Branches git fetch Track/Checkout Remote Branch git checkout --track origin/<brach_name> Renaming Branches # Rename branch locally git branch -m old_branch new_branch # Delete old branch git push origin :old_branch # Push new branch, set local branch to track new remote git push --set-upstream origin new_branch Delete Branches # delete local branch git branch -D <branch_name> # delete remote git push <remote_name> :<branch_name>

item.title: Apache AB
item.description: How to load test Rest APIs with Apache AB.

item.title: K8S Kubectl Quick References
item.description: pretty useful commands for devs building apps in kubernetes.

item.title: NIO File API
item.description: Flat files reading and writing using Java’s NIO API.

item.title: Java JDK Key Tool
item.description: The Java Keytool is a command line tool which can generate public key / private key pairs and store them in a Java KeyStore.

item.title: AWS QLDB & Lambda
item.description: FaaS endpoints to perform actions on AWS QLDB .

item.title: EIP - Enterprise Integration Patterns
item.description: Designing system for enterprise integration references. References https://www.enterpriseintegrationpatterns.com/patterns/messaging/toc.html

item.title: Dependency Inversion Principle
item.description: Dependency Inversion Principle is the third principle of SOLID.

item.title: Interface Segregation Principle
item.description: Interface Segregation Principle in java is the fourth principle of SOLID.

item.title: Liskov Substitution Principle
item.description: Liskov Substitution Principle is the third principle of SOLID.

item.title: Open Closed Principle
item.description: Open Closed Principle is the second principle of SOLID.

item.title: Single Responsibility Principle
item.description: The Single Responsibility Principle says that a object should handle only one thing well.

item.title: DateTime - Duplicates
item.description: Finding duplicate elements in a list of objects, filtering them out by date and time.

item.title: Duplicate Dates
item.description: Finding duplicate elements in a list of objects, filtering them out by dates.

item.title: Sun JKS Utility
item.description: Very useful utility provided by Sun to download and install public certificates from a given web server.

item.title: Data Store References
item.description: Weaknesses and strengths of different data stores and when to use each.

item.title: Jenkins REST API
item.description: This is an example of, how we can use the REST API to manage Jenkins Jobs remotely.

item.title: Jenkins Job Periodically
item.description: In order to run a Jenkins Job periodically, all you need to do is reaching your Job Configuration and move to the Build Triggers section.

item.title: Docker Quick References
item.description: pretty useful commands for devs building apps in docker containers.

item.title: Insertion Sort
item.description: Insertion sort is a simple sorting algorithm that works the way we sort playing cards in our hands with a time Complexity of O(n2) on Big O notation.

item.title: Linear Searching
item.description: Linear search method for finding an element within a list.

item.title: Collections, Streams & Lambdas
item.description: Find the first non-repeated value public static void main(String[] args) { List<Integer> nums = List.

item.title: Tree
item.description: A tree data structure can be defined recursively as a collection of nodes (starting at a root node), where each node is a data structure consisting of a value, together with a list of references to nodes (the “children”), with the constraints that no reference is duplicated, and none points to the root.

item.title: Binary Search
item.description: Binary search, is a search algorithm that finds the position of a target value within a sorted array.

item.title: Stacks
item.description: Stack is a data structure which follows a particular order in which the operations are performed.

item.title: Queues
item.description: A Queue is a linear structure which follows a particular order in which the operations are performed.

item.title: Merge Sort
item.description: Sample merge sort algorithm. import java.util.Arrays; /* * Java Program to sort an integer array using merge sort algorithm.

item.title: Bubble Sort
item.description: Bubble Sort is the simplest sorting algorithm that works by repeatedly swapping the adjacent elements if they are in wrong order.

item.title: Quick Sort
item.description: QuickSort is a divide and conquer algorithm. It picks an element as pivot and partitions the given array around the picked pivot.

item.title: Selection Sort
item.description: The selection sort algorithm sorts an array by repeatedly finding the minimum element (considering ascending order) from unsorted part and putting it at the beginning.

item.title: Java Sockets
item.description: Tool to check server’s health host:port through TCP/IP.

item.title: AspectJ
item.description: AspectJ is a Java library for AOP Example pom.

item.title: Regular Expression
item.description: Remove special characters. public static void main(String[] args) { Scanner scan = new Scanner(System.

item.title: Completable Futures
item.description: Using Java future API to fetch many endpoints at once.

item.title: OS – Listing used ports
item.description: Linux # Checking lsof -i :8080 # If the command above returns multiple result, filter it lsof -i :8080 | grep LISTEN # Find out application details ps -ef | grep <PID>

item.title: Big O Notation
item.description: Big-O measures how well an operation will “scale” when you increase the amount of input data it operates on.

item.title: Fibonacci
item.description: Java fibonacci no recursion public void execute() { int a = 0; int b = 1; int c = 0; for (int i=0; i < 10; i++) { c = a + b; System.
